\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Switching Bernoulli}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.SwitchingBernoulli
\section{Overview}

This module proves the Bernoulli version of H\aa stad's switching lemma for
decision-tree depth: under a Bernoulli$(p)$ random restriction with $p \le 1/(40w)$,
a width-$w$ DNF (or CNF) has restricted decision-tree depth exceeding $t$ with
probability at most $(1/2)^t + e^{-np/3}$. It is derived by bridging from the
fixed-size counting switching lemma to the Bernoulli restriction model.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{lemma}[Cardinality of fixed-size restrictions]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.fixedSizeRestrs_card}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrs, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.freeVars, SwitchingLemma2.numSRestrictions}
For $k \le n$, the set of restrictions of $\mathrm{Fin}\,n$ leaving exactly $k$
variables free has cardinality $\binom{n}{k}\,2^{\,n-k}$, i.e.\ it equals
$\mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,k$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Bad fixed-size restrictions match the counting filter]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.fixedSizeRestrs_filter_bad_eq}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrs, SwitchingLemma2.IsBadRestriction, SwitchingLemma2.IsRestriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.numFree, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For a function $f$ and parameters $d, k$, the number of size-$k$ restrictions
$\rho$ with $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ equals the number of restrictions
that are $k$-restrictions and are bad for $f$ at depth $d$ in the counting
switching-lemma sense.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fixed-size switching bound for small $k$]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound_small}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrProb, BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrs, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingBernoulli.fixedSizeRestrs_card, SwitchingBernoulli.fixedSizeRestrs_filter_bad_eq, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.freeVars, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, SwitchingLemma2.switching_lemma, dtDepth}
For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with no repeated variables per term, $0 < n$, and
$5k \le n$, the probability over uniform size-$k$ restrictions that the
restricted function has decision-tree depth exceeding $d$ is at most
$\left(10kw/n\right)^d$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fixed-size switching bound]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrProb, BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrProb_le_one, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound_small, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with $0 < w$, $0 < n$, and $k \le n$, the size-$k$
restriction probability that $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ is at most
$\left(10kw/n\right)^d$. This removes the $5k \le n$ restriction by using the
trivial bound when $5k > n$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Rescaled fixed-size switching bound]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound_rescaled}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.fixedSizeRestrProb, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
The same fixed-size bound written in the form expected by the Bernoulli
restriction-cost theorem: the probability is at most
$\left(5k\cdot(2w)/n\right)^d$, using $10kw/n = 5k\cdot(2w)/n$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Bernoulli switching lemma for DNFs]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf}
\leanok
\uses{BernoulliCost.bernoulli_restriction_cost, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_fixedSize_bound_rescaled, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb_le_one', SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Let $f$ be a width-$w$ DNF ($0 < w$) with no repeated variables per term, and
$0 < n$. For a Bernoulli$(p)$ random restriction with $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ and
$p \le 1$, and for every $t$,
\[
  \Pr_{R_p}\big[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\big]
    \;\le\; \left(\tfrac12\right)^t + e^{-np/3}.
\]
The $(1/2)^t$ term comes from the switching lemma via the Bernoulli cost
reduction, and $e^{-np/3}$ is the Chernoff tail for leaving too many free
variables.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Bernoulli switching lemma for CNFs]
\lean{SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF.eval, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, SwitchingLemmaCNF.cnfToDualDNF_inj, SwitchingLemmaCNF.cnfToDualDNF_nodup, cnfToDualDNF, cnfToDualDNF_eval, cnfToDualDNF_width, dtDepth, dtDepth_neg}
The same statement for a width-$w$ CNF $f$: under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
with $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ and $p \le 1$,
\[
  \Pr_{R_p}\big[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\big]
    \;\le\; \left(\tfrac12\right)^t + e^{-np/3},
\]
obtained from the DNF version via the dual-DNF representation of $f$.
\end{theorem}
